summary: Comprehensive testing of the signup form schema fix (iter137): backend API returns correct 5-field schema (full_name, company_name, job_title, phone, address - no email/password/standalone-country), migration function converts old schema format, and the Create Customer dialog correctly mirrors the signup form configuration. All 17 backend tests passed. All frontend UI flows verified.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/website-settings returns 200 with settings.signup_form_schema
Backend: signup_form_schema has 'address' field (not standalone 'country')
Backend: signup_form_schema has no 'email' or 'password' fields
Backend: signup_form_schema has all 5 required fields: full_name, company_name, job_title, phone, address
Backend: signup_form_schema field count is exactly 5
Backend: GET /api/admin/website-settings returns 200 with correct migrated schema
Backend: Admin schema has 'address' field, no standalone 'country'
Backend: Admin schema has no email or password fields
Backend: full_name field has required=True in schema
Backend: address field has locked=True in schema
Backend: _migrate_signup_schema() converts old schema (with locked 'country') to new format (with 'address')
Backend: POST /api/admin/customers/create succeeds with basic fields
Backend: POST /api/admin/customers/create succeeds with optional fields (company_name, job_title, phone)
Frontend: Create Customer dialog opens with 'Create Customer' button
Frontend: Full Name * (required) field visible in Create Customer dialog
Frontend: Email * (required) field visible in Create Customer dialog
Frontend: Password * (required) field visible in Create Customer dialog
Frontend: Company Name (schema-driven optional) field visible by default
Frontend: Job Title (schema-driven optional) field visible by default
Frontend: Phone (schema-driven optional) field visible by default
Frontend: ADDRESS block shown by default (address.enabled=true in schema): Line1, Line2, City, Postal, Country, State/Province
Frontend: Required * indicators shown for Full Name, Email, Password fields
Frontend: Validation fires on empty submit - 'Required: Full Name, Email, Password' toast shown
Frontend: Dialog stays open when validation fails
Frontend: Auth & Pages > Sign Up Page tile found and opens slide-over
Frontend: FormSchemaBuilder shows exactly 5 fields: Full Name, Company Name, Job Title, Phone, Address
Frontend: No Email field in schema builder
Frontend: No Password field in schema builder
Frontend: No standalone Country field in schema builder
Frontend: End-to-end Create Customer via UI succeeds with 'Customer created' toast
critical_code_review_comments: The signup_form_schema field count hardcoded assertion (test_signup_schema_field_count_correct) may need updating if more fields are added to the schema in the future.

FormSchemaBuilder initializes state from `value` prop but does not update when `value` changes externally (useState with initial value). This is a known React pattern limitation - if the parent updates ws.signup_form_schema, the component will not re-render with the new value unless the component is remounted. Low priority, no user-facing impact since the component is only used inside the SlideOver.
seed_data_creation: Created test customers: TEST_Iter137_Customer_XXXX, TEST_Iter137_Full_XXXX, TEST UI Iter137 XXXX via backend and UI tests. These are prefixed with TEST_ for easy identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login flow: enter 'automate-accounts' as partner code on root page, click Continue, then log in with admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123!. Auth & Pages tab requires mouse.click using bounding box (not force=True) because the tab is outside normal viewport when sidebar has many items. All signup form schema tests pass. The signup_form_schema now correctly has 5 fields: full_name, company_name, job_title, phone, address. The Create Customer dialog is fully schema-driven for optional fields and address block visibility. Migration function _migrate_signup_schema() correctly converts old schema format (with locked country field) to new format (with address field).
